title: astragalus radicans
intro: Astragalus radicans, also known as milk vetch or locoweed, is a type of perennial legume plant native to North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds. The health benefits of Astragalus radicans are said to include immune system support, cardiovascular health, and anti-aging properties, although more research is needed to confirm these effects. Astragalus radicans can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or capsule, and is often combined with other herbs in traditional Chinese medicine formulations.
